serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Kulcha is one piece, weighing approximately 150 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from 'Indian Bread, Chapati' and 'Potato, boiled' from HPB Foodo, considering the description of Kulcha as an unleavened flatbread with potential fillings. The original input values were used as a guide, but adjusted to align with HPB data for similar components.

gi_source.title: GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Estimated based on 'White bread, Indian, chapati' (GI 62) and considering the potential for potato filling (GI 80-90 for boiled potato). A value of 65 is chosen as a conservative estimate for a refined flour flatbread, potentially with starchy fillings.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and saturated fat from ghee/butter.
hypertension.guidance: Kulcha has moderate sodium (267mg/100g) and saturated fat (3.3g/100g); limit intake to 1-2 pieces and request minimal ghee/butter to manage blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat flour base, high carbohydrate content, and medium glycemic index.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kulcha, made from refined wheat flour with a medium GI (65) and high carbohydrate (33.3g/100g), can significantly impact blood sugar levels; choose wholemeal variants or paneer filling over potato, and limit portion size to manage Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ghee/butter and refined carbohydrates.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kulcha contains moderate saturated fat (3.3g/100g) and refined carbohydrates; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume in moderation, opt for minimal ghee, and pair with fiber-rich foods.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined carbohydrates and saturated fat may contribute to inflammation; nightshade ingredients (potato, chili) can be a trigger for some individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Refined flour and ghee in Kulcha may exacerbate inflammation for some individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; consider paneer-filled options, request minimal ghee, and be mindful of nightshade ingredients if sensitive.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kulcha is a traditional Indian unleavened flatbread, typically baked in a tandoor (clay oven) until golden brown and cooked through. It is often brushed with butter or ghee for added flavor and moisture. Kulcha is commonly offered with various savory fillings, such as spiced potato (Aloo), Indian cheese (Paneer), finely chopped onion, or a mixed spice (Masala) blend. The dough is prepared, filled with the chosen stuffing, flattened, and then baked. It has a soft, slightly chewy texture and a rich, savory taste, varying with the filling. Nutritionally, it is a source of carbohydrates and protein, with moderate fat and sodium content. For clinical relevance, its refined flour base and medium glycemic index warrant caution for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, while its sodium and saturated fat content should be considered for hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo for similar Indian breads and components, as exact Kulcha data is not available. GI is an analogous estimate. The description and cultural context are well-established.
notes: Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g were estimated based on similar food items in HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central due to lack of specific data for Kulcha. Vitamin C is naturally zero as it is cooked.
